image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Radar doesn't need light — it listens for radio echoes bouncing off objects.
Context visual: How does it work? Radar sends out pulses of radio waves, like invisible messengers.
Escalation visual: When these waves hit an object, they bounce back, creating echoes.
Evidence visual: By measuring the time it takes for echoes to return, radar calculates distance.
Payoff visual: This means radar can see through fog, revealing what’s ahead, even in zero visibility.
Cta visual: Want to dive deeper into radar tech? Hit subscribe for more science secrets!
